attributes.additional_details: Recently schools have been cracking down on jewelery for no reason, yet the Government website says nothing about the wearing of jewelery is prohibitted -  so why are schools making it an issue? Sometimes these pieces of jewelrey have been on for years before they even starting school and they can find comfort and a sence of identity in these items. Small pieces of jewelrey in a school environment (not PE) have no effect on learning and should be worn and have been no issues in previous years
attributes.background:
Permit small jewelry to support student identity & family ties. Government guidelines don't mandate a ban, and discrete items have zero impact on learning.
​Limits: Small studs, thin hidden necklaces, or one simple bracelet.

rejection.details:
We can only accept petitions about things the Government or Parliament are directly responsible for.

Government guidance states that it is for the governing board of a school to decide rules about appearance and whether there should be a school uniform policy, as well as what that should be, not the UK Government or Parliament.

You can read more about this here: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/school-uniform/school-uniforms
